expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: SKILL.md, README.md, reference.md, and AGENTS docs stay aligned
expectations.evidence: The run explicitly covers SKILL.md/README.md/reference.md, but cannot validate AGENTS alignment: result.md states “No AGENTS documentation content was included in the evidence bundle, so AGENTS alignment cannot be verified” and execution_notes.md repeats “No AGENTS documentation content included… cannot assess ‘AGENTS docs stay aligned.’” Therefore the full case focus (including AGENTS docs) is not covered.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase docs
expectations.evidence: The output is a docs-phase contract alignment review grounded in the provided phase-contract documents and stays within their scope. It summarizes SKILL.md as authoritative for orchestrator behavior (“SKILL.md defines orchestrator behavior; reference.md defines runtime/artifacts/manifests/phase gates”) and compares against README.md/reference.md, noting specific drift (knowledge-pack/qmd additions) without inventing non-evidenced requirements. It also flags evidence limitations (AGENTS missing; reference.md possibly truncated) rather than asserting unsupported conclusions.
